news.title: Fraudulent citations, blamed on AI hallucinations, are becoming more common in research papers
news.summary: “Fabricated” citations that do not reference real academic papers are spreading in the literature, polluting the public record of science, a new study found

news.title: Key takeaways from WHO briefing on hantavirus cruise ship outbreak
news.summary: Here are key takeaways from a WHO briefing on the hantavirus outbreak aboard a cruise ship.

news.title: Seaport’s IPO adventure, obesity pill battles, and Makary’s troubles
news.summary: This week on "The Readout LOUD" podcast: Seaport Therapeutics’ successful IPO, obesity pill battles, and the FDA commissioner's White House troubles.

news.title: Science is becoming less disruptive. Is an aging workforce to blame?
news.summary: Most researchers conduct their more disruptive work early in their careers, but as they age, they tend to abandon that groundbreaking energy

news.title: Official leading CDC’s cruise ship program retires
news.summary: The hantavirus outbreak on the MV Hondius has focused the public's attention on cruise ship safety.

news.title: STAT+: More political interference at the FDA?
news.summary: A political appointee gets in the way of a Sanofi drug's approval, and more from STAT's D.C. Diagnosis newsletter.

news.title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about Sanofi and an FDA voucher, FDA rethinking a rejection, and more
news.summary: Sanofi asked the FDA to pull teplizumab out of a speedy review voucher program after a political appointee disagreed with staff over its approval

news.title: STAT+: FDA revisits a rare cancer treatment it rejected a few months ago
news.summary: Secret drug pricing deals, a virtual AI cancer clinic, and other biotech news from The Readout

news.title: STAT+: OpenAI’s health policy wishlist, CGM news from Dexcom, and more
news.summary: In this edition of STAT Health Tech: A health policy blueprint from OpenAI, CGM updates from Dexcom, and push back against whole body scans.

news.title: STAT+: FDA to reconsider treatment for rare cancer after its surprise rejection
news.summary: The companies developing Ebvallo reached an agreement with the FDA to address its main reason for rejecting the drug.

news.title: Hospital shootings steadily increased since 2000
news.summary: What the FDA has lost, why hantavirus isn't the next pandemic, and more health news from Morning Rounds

news.title: STAT+: Next-gen Duchenne drug from Entrada disappoints
news.summary: A next-gen Duchenne therapy from Entrada Therapeutics fell short in an early study, as a number of companies race to make improved drugs.

news.title: STAT+: Color me skeptical: Drinking gold is not an ALS cure
news.summary: In this week's Biotech Scorecard newsletter, Adam Feuerstein takes on the company touting gold microdust suspended in water as an ALS treatment.

news.title: STAT+: Angelini Pharma buys Catalyst Pharmaceuticals and its rare disease drugs for $4.1B
news.summary: The Italian firm Angelini Pharma is buying Catalyst Pharmaceuticals, the maker of rare disease drugs, for $4.1 billion.

news.title: Cruise ship hantavirus outbreak worries experts, though they discount pandemic fears
news.summary: Scientists are worried that hantaviruses haven’t been as well studied as they ought to be, but not that the MV Hondius cruise ship is ground zero for the next pandemic.

news.title: Amazon Pharmacy to offer home delivery for Novo Nordisk's Ozempic pill
news.summary: Amazon Pharmacy will make Novo Nordisk's Ozempic pill available for home delivery, the company announced Thursday.

news.title: Staffing firm Cross Country Healthcare to be acquired by Knox Lane for $437M
news.summary: The deal would bring the 40-year-old, publicly traded company under a growth-oriented investment firm with a prior background in healthcare staffing.

news.title: Clover Health's MA membership grows 51% year-over-year
news.summary: Clover Health's membership climbed 51% year-over-year in the first quarter, reaching 155,773, per its earnings report released Wednesday.

news.title: Pennsylvania sues Character.ai over AI chatbot allegedly presenting itself as licensed medical professional
news.summary: The state's suit alleges Character.ai violated the state's Medical Practice Act after an AI chatbot falsely claimed to be a licensed psychiatrist, even providing a fake license number.

news.title: Hims & Hers debuts its first AI care agent to interpret biomarker lab results
news.summary: Labs AI is a custom-built AI system that reasons across customers’ biomarker histories and personal health profiles to produce insights that are specific, safe and actionable.

news.title: Oscar Health's profit hits $679M, membership rises in Q1
news.summary: Oscar Health is seeing some favorable signs on the embattled individual market.

news.title: Ardent Health touts outpatient growth, checked labor spend during hectic Q1
news.summary: The publicly traded for-profit beat the consensus revenue and earnings estimates and said it now has increased confidence in hitting its full-year financial targets.

news.title: Joint Commission, NACHC partner on training, new accreditation for community health centers
news.summary: The duo will develop education, training and advisory services for community health centers. A new accreditation program will also be created.

news.title: Nearly 8 in 10 employers say GLP-1 coverage drives up benefit costs: Business Group on Health
news.summary: Seventy-two percent of respondents said they would likely continue GLP-1 coverage through 2027 while 10% do not expect to continue coverage.

news.title: PeaceHealth, physician group agree to 'reset' litigious ED staffing dispute
news.summary: A joint statement appears to signal an upcoming deal in a lawsuit over a staffing decision that drew national attention as an early challenge to Oregon's recent statute restricting corporate medicine.

news.title: Leapfrog Group's latest safety grades have far fewer low scores since removal of non-participating hospitals
news.summary: The patient safety group spotlighted broad improvements in most patient safety and experience measures among the first set of grades following a court-ordered removal of hospitals that declined to voluntarily submit data.

news.title: CVS execs say company on track to meet MA margin goals by 2028
news.summary: CVS Health beat the Street on both earnings and revenue in Q1, posting $2.9 billion in profit for the quarter.

news.title: Chutes & Ladders—Biopharma veteran takes her CEO Cue
news.summary: Not long after the departure of former CEO Usman Azam, a new leader is taking her Cue in the form of Shao-Lee Lin, M.D., Ph.D. Lin steps into Cue’s president and chief exec spot after previously serving as founder and CEO of eye disease specialist Acelyrin, which later merged with Alumis.

news.title: Banyan BioInnovations puts down roots with $100M, Icon partnership
news.summary: Banyan BioInnovations is debuting with more than $100 million aimed at developing NewCos around promising clinical assets, plus a partnership with global contract research organization Icon.

news.title: BeOne shelves 5 cancer programs, halts phase 2 rheumatoid arthritis trial
news.summary: BeOne Medicines has shaken up its early pipeline, dropping five phase 1 cancer programs and stopping a phase 2 arthritis trial based on new data.

news.title: Antibodies that stop measles from stabbing cells could become new treatments for the surging virus, study suggests
news.summary: Though a measles vaccine has existed for more than 60 years, much still remains unknown about how exactly the shot generates protection against the virus. Now, scientists have turned to the immune systems of vaccinated people to dig up answers—and their findings may lead to new medicines against the resurgent disease.

news.title: FDA to reconsider shock rejection of cell therapy Ebvallo. Could uniQure be next?
news.summary: After a surprise rejection in January, the FDA has agreed to reconsider a T-cell therapy based on the same single-arm trial that the agency had previously found problematic. The case could reignite investors’ hope of a potential reversal for uniQure's high-profile gene therapy, too.

news.title: Blackstone invests $250M in Anagram to reduce burden of cystic fibrosis complication
news.summary: For people living with exocrine pancreatic insufficiency due to cystic fibrosis, the only treatment option is a regimen that can include up to 40 pills a day. With a $250 million investment from Blackstone Life Sciences, Anagram Therapeutics hopes to reduce that pill burden to just three pills daily.

news.title: Ascendis axes IL-2 program in retreat from oncology expansion
news.summary: Ascendis Pharma has ended internal development of its IL-2 candidate, pivoting away from oncology and calling time on an asset it once tipped for accelerated approval.

news.title: Roche shells out up to $1B for PathAI in move to boost artificial intelligence-powered diagnostics
news.summary: Roche is set to pay out a little more than $1 billion to acquire digital pathology specialist Path AI in a move the pharma giant hopes will bolster the use of artificial intelligence in diagnostic algorithms.

news.title: Entrada's stock craters as DMD data underperform expectations, handing advantage to Novartis
news.summary: Entrada Therapeutics’ Duchenne muscular dystrophy data have fallen well short of expectations, crashing the biotech’s stock as investors evaluated the fallout from a key readout expected to validate its platform.

news.title: From drug development to M&A, Big Pharmas showcase AI’s ‘measurable impact’
news.summary: The recent run of first-quarter earnings results marked a shift for Big Pharmas away from AI hype and toward explaining how the technology is being applied in practice.

news.title: Insulet boosts guidance in Q1 update as it grapples with recall saga
news.summary: Insulet is upping its guidance after a strong first quarter, though ongoing recalls continue to cast a shadow for investors.

news.title: Ipsen scuttles liver disease candidates from $952M Albireo acquisition
news.summary: Ipsen has set aside the two drug candidates it picked up from a 2023 acquisition of liver disease specialist Albireo, with one of the assets discontinued entirely.

news.title: 科技正在重塑“睡眠呼吸暂停”的治疗方式
news.summary: 一大批全新疗法开始走入大众视野。

news.title: DeepSleep（好睡乐）完成数百万元天使轮融资，用“无感干预”重塑睡眠赛道 | 36氪首发
news.summary: 用“无感干预”重塑睡眠赛道。

papers.title: SWE-WebDevBench: Evaluating Coding Agent Application Platforms as Virtual Software Agencies
papers.summary: The emergence of "vibe coding" platforms, where users describe applications in natural language and AI agents autonomously generate full-stack software, has created a need for rigorous evaluation beyond code-level benchmarks. In order to assess them as virtual software development agencies on understanding business requirements, making architectural decisions, writing production code, handling iterative modifications, and maintaining business readiness, we introduce SWE-WebDev Bench, a 68-metric evaluation framework spanning 25 primary and 43 diagnostic metrics across seven groups, organized a

papers.title: MedSkillAudit: A Domain-Specific Audit Framework for Medical Research Agent Skills
papers.summary: Background: Agent skills are increasingly deployed as modular, reusable capability units in AI agent systems. Medical research agent skills require safeguards beyond general-purpose evaluation, including scientific integrity, methodological validity, reproducibility, and boundary safety. This study developed and preliminarily evaluated a domain-specific audit framework for medical research agent skills, with a focus on reliability against expert review. Methods: We developed MedSkillAudit (skill-auditor@1.0), a layered framework assessing skill release readiness before deployment. We evaluated
